title: Interac eГўВҖВ‘TransferГӮВ® Online Administration System (OAS)
body_text:
Interac
eГўВҖВ‘Transfer
ГӮВ®
Online Administration System (OAS)
1	Introduction to
Interac
eГўВҖВ‘Transfer Online Administration System (OAS)
1.1	Overview of the
Interac
eГўВҖВ‘Transfer Online Administration System (OAS)
The
Interac
e-Transfer
ГӮВ®
Online Administration System (OAS) allows financial-institution-authorized users to monitor and trace their customersГўВҖВҷ
Interac
e-Transfer transactions.
Authorized users can view the following details in OAS:
Status or state of your customersГўВҖВҷ
Interac
e-Transfer transactions (e.g., Blocked, Active, Received)
Your customersГўВҖВҷ
Interac
e-Transfer profile details (name, email address, etc.)
Your customersГўВҖВҷ
Interac
e-Transfer history (transaction history, creation date, etc.)
Your customersГўВҖВҷ Autodeposit registrations
OAS is available to participating financial institutions to supplement the tracing of
Interac
eГўВҖВ‘Transfer transactions using reconciliation files. For information about tracing
Interac
e-Transfer transactions using reconciliation files, refer to the
Interac
e-Transfer Limits, Settlement, Tracing, and Reports Part.
For financial institutions that have been granted use of the OAS Fraud Analyst role by Central 1, OAS can also be used to manage
Interac
eГўВҖВ‘Transfer transactions that are flagged as suspected fraud. The OAS Fraud Analyst role is described in Chapter 6, The OAS Fraud Analyst Role.
Note:
Interac
e-Transfer data is available on OAS for two years. Data related to
Interac
eГўВҖВ‘Transfer transactions older than two years is only available in OAS through an archived data search. Refer to Section 4.8, Archived Data Searches.
Note:	Financial institutions not participating in OAS continue solely using reconciliation files for tracing their
Interac
e-Transfer transactions.
